title: On options-driven realized volatility forecasting: Information gains via rough volatility model
summary: This academic paper examines whether model-based spot volatility estimators extracted from traded options data improve predictive power of the Heterogeneous Autoregressive (HAR) model for realized volatility. The authors infer spot volatility under a rough stochastic volatility model using an iterative two-step approach with deep learning acceleration. Their augmented HAR-RV-RHeston model demonstrates improved daily realized volatility forecasting accuracy compared to traditional stochastic volatility models (Heston, Bates, SVCJ) and the VIX index, with superior performance sustained across horizons up to one month.
key_points: Paper proposes augmenting HAR model with rough volatility-based spot volatility estimators from options data
Uses iterative two-step approach following Andersen et al. (2015a) for spot volatility inference
Deep learning surrogate employed to accelerate model estimation from large-scale options panels
Benchmarks against Heston, Bates, SVCJ stochastic volatility models and VIX index
Augmented HAR-RV-RHeston model shows improved daily realized volatility forecasting accuracy
Superior performance sustained across forecasting horizons up to one month
why_it_matters: This is academic quantitative finance research on volatility modeling methodology. It is not directly relevant to market structure or macro catalysts as defined for this archive. It may be of interest to researchers and practitioners working on volatility forecasting models, but does not inform market structure dynamics or macro policy decisions.
